title: After the Oka Sho: This Doesn’t Feel Right!

text_block_list.name: Sweep Tosho
text_block_list.text: Pant, pant, pant!

text_block_list.name: Sweep Tosho
text_block_list.text:
(So this is a Triple Tiara race... Everyone here
only gets this one chance to win it.)

text_block_list.name: Sweep Tosho
text_block_list.text: (...I guess it does feel a little special. But...)

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text: Wooooo!

text_block_list.name: Fan A
text_block_list.text:
What a fantastic race! This year's battle for the
Triple Tiara has finally kicked off!

text_block_list.name: Fan B
text_block_list.text:
You said it! I hope we get to see a bunch of
elegant and spectacular races this year as well.

text_block_list.name: Fan C
text_block_list.text:
The spring Classics are just getting started! Next
week is the long-awaited Satsuki Sho!

text_block_list.name: Spectator D
text_block_list.text:
That's the race that decides who the fastest
Umamusume is! There's no way I'm missing it!

text_block_list.name: Sweep Tosho
text_block_list.text: Ugh! Seriously?!

text_block_list.name: Sweep Tosho
text_block_list.text: Why is the Satsuki Sho the only race that can settle who the fastest Umamusume is?!

text_block_list.name: Sweep Tosho
text_block_list.text: Aaagh! I'm sick of it! It just doesn't feel right!

text_block_list.name: Sweep Tosho
text_block_list.text:
It's still not ready yet! My racing magic is nowhere
near complete!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Some time later, all the Umamusume on the Triple
Tiara tour—including Sweep Tosho—are gathered
together for an interview about their future plans.

text_block_list.name: Umamusume with Blue Ribbons
text_block_list.text:
Yes, naturally, I intend to run in the Japanese
Oaks. I hope I can count on your continued
support!

text_block_list.name: Reporters
text_block_list.text: Clap, clap, clap!

text_block_list.name: Reporter Otonashi
text_block_list.text:
Next up, we have Sweep Tosho and her trainer.
Thank you for agreeing to today's interview.

text_block_list.name: Reporter Otonashi
text_block_list.text:
First, perhaps you could tell us about your future
prospects and goals?

text_block_list.name: Sweep Tosho
text_block_list.text:
Hmph. Well, there's no use talking about dumb
things like prospects and goals...

text_block_list.name: Sweep Tosho
text_block_list.text:
Because someday, I'm gonna cast my magic on
each and every one of you!

text_block_list.name: Sweep Tosho
text_block_list.text:
And then everyone's lame assumptions are going
out the window! When I'm done, nobody's ever
gonna say "that's the way things are" again!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text: Chatter, chatter...

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
The crowd seems slightly shaken. I feel the weight
of countless stares, and an immense pressure to
step in and stop her.
choice_data_list: (...But I'm not going to stop her.)

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
After all, this is Sweep Tosho's dream—her
ultimate goal. And I want to respect that.

text_block_list.name: Reporter Otonashi
text_block_list.text: I see... Your "magic," you say?

text_block_list.name: Reporter Otonashi
text_block_list.text:
And do you mind if I ask what you were referring
to when you mentioned "everyone's lame
assumptions"?

text_block_list.name: Sweep Tosho
text_block_list.text:
Stuff like how Umamusume on the Triple Crown
tour are supposedly stronger than Umamusume
on the Triple Tiara tour!

text_block_list.name: Sweep Tosho
text_block_list.text:
Strong runners are strong no matter what tour
they're on! Is it really such a big deal if someone
from the Tiara tour beats someone from the
Triple Crown? And how come it's the Satsuki Sho
and not the Oka Sho that settles who's fastest?

text_block_list.name: Sweep Tosho
text_block_list.text: All that stuff is lame! Laaame!

text_block_list.name: Reporter Otonashi
text_block_list.text: ...I see. I know precisely what you mean.

text_block_list.name: Reporter Otonashi
text_block_list.text:
Although... at the risk of sounding rude, might I
ask one more question?

text_block_list.name: Sweep Tosho
text_block_list.text: What is it?

text_block_list.name: Reporter Otonashi
text_block_list.text:
Speaking very frankly, the results of many
races—such as the Arima Kinen and the
Takarazuka Kinen—support those views.

text_block_list.name: Reporter Otonashi
text_block_list.text:
When a runner on the Triple Crown tour and a
runner on the Triple Tiara tour clash during
senior-year races, for example, the latter almost
always loses.

text_block_list.name: Reporter Otonashi
text_block_list.text:
You could even say that history has proven it...
What are your thoughts on that?

text_block_list.name: Sweep Tosho
text_block_list.text:
In that case, I'm just gonna go ahead and change
the course of history myself!

text_block_list.name: Sweep Tosho
text_block_list.text:
I'll give you my own proof! It doesn't matter what
tour a runner's on—strength is strength!

text_block_list.name: Sweep Tosho
text_block_list.text:
First, I'm gonna win the Triple Tiara! And then, in
my senior year...

text_block_list.name: Sweep Tosho
text_block_list.text:
I'm gonna beat the Triple Crown tour runners and
show the whole world how tough I am!

text_block_list.name: Sweep Tosho
text_block_list.text:
But listen up, reporters. If any of you write some
lame article about how an Umamusume from the
Triple Tiara tour amazingly won or whatever, I'm
gonna come for you personally.

text_block_list.name: Sweep Tosho
text_block_list.text:
All you need to write is, "Sweep Tosho was the
strongest!" That's it!

text_block_list.name: Reporter Otonashi
text_block_list.text:
Ah, I see. So that's the magic you're hoping to
show everyone.

text_block_list.name: Reporter Otonashi
text_block_list.text:
It doesn't matter what tour an Umamusume's
on—strength is strength. And you're going to
prove it in your senior year!

text_block_list.name: Reporter Otonashi
text_block_list.text:
I'm looking forward to your upcoming races.
Thank you very much!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text: Chatter, chatter...

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Otonashi seems to have grasped what Sweep
Tosho was getting at, but the whole room remains
astir with confusion.

text_block_list.name: Sweep Tosho
text_block_list.text:
Hmph. Familiar, you dummy. I just did what
anyone would!

text_block_list.name: Reporter Otonashi
text_block_list.text:
"Familiar"?! Oh, what a wonderful relationship! Is
that a sign of how devoted you are to her?!

text_block_list.name: Reporter Otonashi
text_block_list.text:
And I'll bet you're ready to run through fire,
water, controversy, and accursed ground at her
command!
choice_data_list:
(That sounds like a lot of running... Hm, I
wonder.)

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Otonashi tends to exaggerate our hardships, but
as I ponder our seemingly perilous future, I can't
help thinking she might be on the money this
time.

text_block_list.name: Sweep Tosho
text_block_list.text:
All right, Familiar! No dilly-dallying! Let's get to
work!

text_block_list.name: Sweep Tosho
text_block_list.text:
I'm gonna run in that special race and complete
my magic! So I need you to train me up for the
Japanese Oaks!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Our next goal is the second race of the Triple
Tiara tour—the Japanese Oaks. A fierce battle to
decide the Queen of the Oaks awaits our little
witch!

text_block_list.name: Umamusume with Blue Ribbons
text_block_list.text: ..."Magic"? How ridiculous.

text_block_list.name: Umamusume with Blue Ribbons
text_block_list.text:
And she's already talking about her senior year...
Is she trying to represent our generation or
something?

text_block_list.name: Umamusume with Blue Ribbons
text_block_list.text: Ugh... that's my role to play!
